name: Masquerading as Linux Crond Process
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Masquerading as Linux Crond Process
SIGMA Description: Masquerading occurs when the name or location of an executable, legitimate or malicious, is manipulated or abused for the sake of evading defenses and observation.
Several different variations of this technique have been observed.


This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%cp%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%/bin/sh%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a2%/crond%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Masquerading as Linux Crond Process on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%cp%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%/bin/sh%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a2%/crond%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Masquerading as Linux Crond Process

This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
